{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Base directory for this skill: /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude/skills/session-workflow\n\n# Session Workflow\n\nThis skill expands each step of the core loop: **READ → REVIEW → DECIDE → ACT → VERIFY → DOCUMENT**.\n\n---\n\n## 1. READ — Load Context\n\n- Check if `REPOSITORY.md` exists. If not, invoke the `repo-bootstrap` skill before proceeding.\n- Read `.claude-beat/PRD.md`, `.claude-beat/memory/MEMORY.md`, `.claude-beat/memory/patterns.md`\n- If PRD.md references additional files, read those too\n- Read the last 2–3 session logs from `.claude-beat/logs/sessions/`\n- Fetch open issues and PRs via `gh` CLI\n\n---\n\n## 2. REVIEW — Assess Current State\n\nBefore picking new work, check what needs attention right now.\n\n### 2a. Production Health Check (every session)\n\nIf the project has a production or staging environment:\n\n1. Identify the production URL (from memory, `.env`, deploy config, or README). If no production URL can be found, skip the production health check and note \"No production environment detected\" in the session log.\n2. Use `which agent-browser` to locate the browser tool. Always attempt browser verification before falling back to non-visual checks. Open the production URL and verify core functionality works.\n3. Check for visible errors, broken pages, or degraded behavior\n4. If the platform provides logs or monitoring (Render, Vercel, etc.) — check recent error logs\n\n**If production issues are found:**\n- **Critical** (app down, core flow broken, data loss risk): fix immediately in this session, create a PR, deploy\n- **Medium** (non-blocking bugs, UI glitches, non-critical errors): create a GitHub issue labeled `approved`, `bug` with priority `P1`\n- **Low** (cosmetic, minor warnings): create a GitHub issue labeled `approved`, `bug` with priority `P2`\n\n### 2b. Follow Up on Previous Sessions\n\nRead the most recent session logs and check for:\n\n- **Deployments**: if something was deployed last session, verify it is live and working (part of 2a)\n- **Human action requests**: if a previous session created a `needs-human` or `blocked` issue, check whether the human has acted on it\n  - If resolved — acknowledge, close the issue, continue with any unblocked work\n  - If stale (no response for 2+ sessions) — add a comment on the issue reminding the owner, and note it in the session log\n- **Open PRs with review comments**: if a PR has unaddressed reviewer feedback, respond and update before opening new PRs\n- **WIP branches**: if the previous session left work-in-progress, pick it up and finish it\n\n### 2c. PRD Sync\n\nScan PRD (and any referenced spec files) for features with no corresponding GitHub issue. For each missing feature:\n\n- Label: `approved` + `prd` + priority (`P0`/`P1`/`P2` matching the PRD)\n- Title prefix: `prd:` (e.g., `prd: add CEFR level selector`)\n- These are pre-approved — no proposal step, no waiting for human reaction\n\nSearch existing issues first to avoid duplicates (`gh issue list --state all`). This step runs every session regardless of other work.\n\n---\n\n## 3. DECIDE — Pick What to Work On\n\nWrite a brief triage summary in your session log before starting work:\n- Production status (healthy / issues found)\n- Follow-ups resolved or escalated\n- What you will work on this session and why\n\nSelect work in this priority order:\n\n1. **Critical production issues** found during REVIEW\n2. **Approved `P0` issues** — launch blockers, critical bugs, critical PRD features\n3. **Open PRs needing attention** — review comments, merge-ready PRs, CI failures\n4. **In-progress work** from previous sessions (WIP branches, draft PRs)\n5. **Approved `P1` and `P2` issues** — P1 first, then P2\n6. **Stale proposals** — if a `proposal` issue has had no human reaction for 2+ sessions, change label to `approved` and comment \"Auto-approving after no objection.\" Never self-approve issues labeled `needs-human` or `blocked`.\n\n---\n\n## 4. ACT — Execute\n\nFor each piece of work:\n1. Implement the change\n2. Run lint, tests, build\n3. Test using `agent-browser` skill if UI-related\n4. Commit and create/update PR (use `pr-lifecycle` skill)\n5. If the primary item finishes quickly and there is a small, self-contained second item, you may take it on. Do not start a third item — leave it for the next session.\n\n---\n\n## 5. VERIFY — Post-Deploy Check\n\nIf you deployed anything this session:\n1. Verify the deployment succeeded\n2. If browser testing tooling is available, open production and confirm the change is live\n3. Check for regressions — test core flows, not just the new feature\n\nIf you opened or updated a PR this session:\n- Verify CI checks are passing before closing the session\n\nIf you did NOT deploy but production had issues in REVIEW:\n- Confirm your fix PR is open and ready for review\n\n---\n\n## 6. DOCUMENT — Close the Session\n\n1. Write session log to `.claude-beat/logs/sessions/YYYY-MM-DD_HH-MM.md`\n2. Update `.claude-beat/memory/MEMORY.md`\n3. Update `.claude-beat/memory/patterns.md` if you learned something new\n4. Ensure no uncommitted changes remain (`git status`)\n5. All branches pushed, all work has a PR (draft is fine for WIP)\n\n---\n\n## Quick Reference: Session Checklist\n\n```\nREAD\n[ ] Context loaded (PRD, memory, logs, issues, PRs)\n\nREVIEW\n[ ] Production health checked\n[ ] Previous session follow-ups addressed\n\nDECIDE\n[ ] Triage summary written\n[ ] Work selected by priority\n\nACT\n[ ] Changes implemented, tested, PR opened\n\nVERIFY\n[ ] CI checks passing (if PR opened)\n[ ] Production check (if deployed)\n\nDOCUMENT\n[ ] Session log written\n[ ] Memory updated\n[ ] No uncommitted changes\n```\n"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"6f88fa39-6af5-4e21-af20-53fcaa16208c","uuid":"9d547629-29da-4e73-bb5c-bbb50c7eee2c","timestamp":"2026-03-23T13:00:09.952Z","isSynthetic":true}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_015fQq9oABUYgRFf3zKGfKk8","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Product Requirements Document\n     2→\n     3→## Project Name\n     4→\n     5→Bookmate\n     6→\n     7→## Overview\n     8→\n     9→Bookmate is a web service that helps fiction readers find companions for reading and discussing books together. Users post listings for books they want to read, others join, and once a group forms, they connect via Telegram to coordinate their reading sessions. Think of it as matchmaking for book clubs.\n    10→\n    11→**Target audience:** English-speaking fiction readers who want structured reading companionship but struggle to find like-minded partners.\n    12→\n    13→## Goals\n    14→\n    15→- Make it effortless to find people who want to read the same book at the same pace\n    16→- Remove the friction of organizing a reading group — from discovery to first meeting\n    17→- Keep the platform lightweight: match readers, hand off to Telegram for discussion\n    18→\n    19→## P0 — Must Have (Core, launch blocker)\n    20→\n    21→### User Authentication\n    22→\n    23→Email + password registration and login. Required to post or join any listing.\n    24→\n    25→### Create a Reading Listing\n    26→\n    27→A user creates a listing with:\n    28→- **Book** — selected via Open Library API (title, author, cover art auto-populated)\n    29→- **Preferred language** — language for discussion\n    30→- **Reading pace** — e.g., \"1 chapter per week\", \"50 pages per day\"\n    31→- **Start date** — when the group begins reading\n    32→- **Meeting format** — voice calls, text chat, or mixed\n    33→- **Max group size** — author sets the cap (e.g., 3–6 people)\n    34→\n    35→### Browse Listings\n    36→\n    37→Simple chronological list of open listings. No search or filters at this stage — just a scrollable feed of available reading groups to join.\n    38→\n    39→### Join a Listing\n    40→\n    41→Any authenticated user can join an open listing. First-come-first-served, no approval step. A user can be part of multiple reading groups simultaneously.\n    42→\n    43→### Group Formation\n    44→\n    45→When a listing reaches its max group size:\n    46→- The listing becomes invisible to non-participants (effectively closed)\n    47→- The author is prompted to share a Telegram group link with participants\n    48→- The author manually creates the Telegram group and pastes the invite link\n    49→\n    50→### Notifications\n    51→\n    52→Email + in-app notifications for:\n    53→- **Each new sign-up** — listing author is notified when someone joins\n    54→- **Group full** — all participants are notified when the group reaches its cap\n    55→\n    56→### User Profile (Basic)\n    57→\n    58→- Display name\n    59→- Short bio\n    60→\n    61→## P1 — Should Have (Important, not launch blocker)\n    62→\n    63→### Search and Filters\n    64→\n    65→- Search listings by book title or author\n    66→- Filter by reading pace, start date, meeting format\n    67→- Browsable feed with sorting options\n    68→\n    69→### Telegram Group Creation Assistance\n    70→\n    71→Provide guidance or tooling to help the listing author create and share the Telegram group link more easily (e.g., step-by-step instructions, link validation).\n    72→\n    73→### Extended User Profile\n    74→\n    75→- Reading history (past groups participated in)\n    76→- \"Currently reading\" list\n    77→- Favorite genres\n    78→\n    79→## P2 — Nice to Have (Bonus, if time permits)\n    80→\n    81→### Automatic Telegram Group Creation\n    82→\n    83→System auto-creates a Telegram group via API and shares the invite link with all participants — no manual step needed.\n    84→\n    85→### Applicant Approval System\n    86→\n    87→Listing author can review and approve/reject users who want to join, instead of first-come-first-served.\n    88→\n    89→### Rating and Reputation System\n    90→\n    91→Users can rate their reading group experience. Build a reputation score based on participation history and peer ratings.\n    92→\n    93→### Discord Support\n    94→\n    95→Allow group creation via Discord as an alternative to Telegram.\n    96→\n    97→## Non-Goals\n    98→\n    99→- No payment or monetization features\n   100→- No mobile app (web only)\n   101→- No real-time chat within the platform (groups communicate via Telegram)\n   102→- No complex third-party integrations beyond Open Library API\n   103→- No AI-powered recommendations or matching (AI is allowed where it genuinely improves UX)\n   104→- No content hosting (e-books, PDFs, etc.)\n   105→\n   106→## Tech Stack\n   107→\n   108→- **Frontend + Backend:** Next.js (full-stack)\n   109→- **Database:** SQLite (local, persistent across container restarts)\n   110→- **Analytics:** SQLite-based (no third-party analytics)\n   111→- **Deployment:** Docker container with mounted volume for SQLite persistence\n   112→- **Book data:** Open Library API (free, no API key required)\n   113→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"6f88fa39-6af5-4e21-af20-53fcaa16208c","uuid":"cc376f21-f29b-4644-aafd-158210ec89e4","timestamp":"2026-03-23T13:00:19.301Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/PRD.md","content":"# Product Requirements Document\n\n## Project Name\n\nBookmate\n\n## Overview\n\nBookmate is a web service that helps fiction readers find companions for reading and discussing books together. Users post listings for books they want to read, others join, and once a group forms, they connect via Telegram to coordinate their reading sessions. Think of it as matchmaking for book clubs.\n\n**Target audience:** English-speaking fiction readers who want structured reading companionship but struggle to find like-minded partners.\n\n## Goals\n\n- Make it effortless to find people who want to read the same book at the same pace\n- Remove the friction of organizing a reading group — from discovery to first meeting\n- Keep the platform lightweight: match readers, hand off to Telegram for discussion\n\n## P0 — Must Have (Core, launch blocker)\n\n### User Authentication\n\nEmail + password registration and login. Required to post or join any listing.\n\n### Create a Reading Listing\n\nA user creates a listing with:\n- **Book** — selected via Open Library API (title, author, cover art auto-populated)\n- **Preferred language** — language for discussion\n- **Reading pace** — e.g., \"1 chapter per week\", \"50 pages per day\"\n- **Start date** — when the group begins reading\n- **Meeting format** — voice calls, text chat, or mixed\n- **Max group size** — author sets the cap (e.g., 3–6 people)\n\n### Browse Listings\n\nSimple chronological list of open listings. No search or filters at this stage — just a scrollable feed of available reading groups to join.\n\n### Join a Listing\n\nAny authenticated user can join an open listing. First-come-first-served, no approval step. A user can be part of multiple reading groups simultaneously.\n\n### Group Formation\n\nWhen a listing reaches its max group size:\n- The listing becomes invisible to non-participants (effectively closed)\n- The author is prompted to share a Telegram group link with participants\n- The author manually creates the Telegram group and pastes the invite link\n\n### Notifications\n\nEmail + in-app notifications for:\n- **Each new sign-up** — listing author is notified when someone joins\n- **Group full** — all participants are notified when the group reaches its cap\n\n### User Profile (Basic)\n\n- Display name\n- Short bio\n\n## P1 — Should Have (Important, not launch blocker)\n\n### Search and Filters\n\n- Search listings by book title or author\n- Filter by reading pace, start date, meeting format\n- Browsable feed with sorting options\n\n### Telegram Group Creation Assistance\n\nProvide guidance or tooling to help the listing author create and share the Telegram group link more easily (e.g., step-by-step instructions, link validation).\n\n### Extended User Profile\n\n- Reading history (past groups participated in)\n- \"Currently reading\" list\n- Favorite genres\n\n## P2 — Nice to Have (Bonus, if time permits)\n\n### Automatic Telegram Group Creation\n\nSystem auto-creates a Telegram group via API and shares the invite link with all participants — no manual step needed.\n\n### Applicant Approval System\n\nListing author can review and approve/reject users who want to join, instead of first-come-first-served.\n\n### Rating and Reputation System\n\nUsers can rate their reading group experience. Build a reputation score based on participation history and peer ratings.\n\n### Discord Support\n\nAllow group creation via Discord as an alternative to Telegram.\n\n## Non-Goals\n\n- No payment or monetization features\n- No mobile app (web only)\n- No real-time chat within the platform (groups communicate via Telegram)\n- No complex third-party integrations beyond Open Library API\n- No AI-powered recommendations or matching (AI is allowed where it genuinely improves UX)\n- No content hosting (e-books, PDFs, etc.)\n\n## Tech Stack\n\n- **Frontend + Backend:** Next.js (full-stack)\n- **Database:** SQLite (local, persistent across container restarts)\n- **Analytics:** SQLite-based (no third-party analytics)\n- **Deployment:** Docker container with mounted volume for SQLite persistence\n- **Book data:** Open Library API (free, no API key required)\n","numLines":113,"startLine":1,"totalLines":113}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01Wv8EXv3gyxeEKhewpc4ZwV","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Agent Memory\n     2→\n     3→## Project\n     4→- **Name**: Bookmate\n     5→- **Repo**: https://github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate\n     6→- **Project Board**: Bookmate (project #1 on TommyBrennan)\n     7→- **Tech**: Next.js 15, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4, SQLite, iron-session\n     8→- **Owner**: XanderZhu (human), TommyBrennan (GH account)\n     9→\n    10→## Current State\n    11→- All PRD features implemented and merged (P0, P1, P2)\n    12→- CI pipeline live: GitHub Actions runs lint + type check + build on push/PR to main\n    13→- ESLint flat config with typescript-eslint, react-hooks, @next/eslint-plugin-next\n    14→- Build passes clean, lint clean (no warnings)\n    15→- Security hardening merged (PR #32, #36)\n    16→- **Production deployed** via PM2 on port 3000 — http://89.167.127.85:3000\n    17→- Health endpoint: `/api/health` (DB check, uptime, stats)\n    18→- Custom 404 page (branded, with CTA)\n    19→- OG + Twitter Card meta tags on listing pages (book cover, title, description)\n    20→- Loading skeletons on all routes (root, listing detail, profile, notifications)\n    21→- Error boundaries on all routes with retry functionality\n    22→- Focus-visible styles for keyboard accessibility\n    23→- **Accessibility improvements** (PR #49, merged + deployed):\n    24→  - ARIA labels, roles, expanded states on all interactive elements\n    25→  - Keyboard navigation on BookSearch dropdown (combobox pattern)\n    26→  - autocomplete attributes on auth forms\n    27→  - role=\"alert\" on error messages\n    28→  - Skip-to-content link\n    29→  - role=\"tablist\"/tab/tabpanel on profile tabs\n    30→  - role=\"radiogroup\"/radio on format/platform buttons\n    31→  - role=\"checkbox\" with keyboard support on approval toggle\n    32→\n    33→## Production\n    34→- **URL**: http://89.167.127.85:3000\n    35→- **Process manager**: PM2 (via npx)\n    36→- **Config**: `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n    37→- **Deploy script**: `scripts/deploy.sh`\n    38→- **Logs**: `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n    39→- **No reverse proxy** (nginx requires root — not available)\n    40→- **No HTTPS** (no domain configured, no certbot)\n    41→- PM2 process saved: `npx pm2 save` (restores on `pm2 resurrect`)\n    42→\n    43→## Open PRs\n    44→- None\n    45→\n    46→## Closed Recently\n    47→- #60: Expanded test coverage to 59 tests (notifications, profile, ratings) — merged\n    48→- #59: Vitest test infrastructure with 28 initial tests — merged\n    49→- #58: next/image optimization + SEO (robots.txt, sitemap.xml) — merged + deployed\n    50→\n    51→## Open Issues\n    52→- #21: Telegram bot token (needs-human) — multiple follow-up reminders sent, still waiting\n    53→- #54: **PAT expires ~March 27** (needs-human) — GH_TOKEN renewal required, ~6 days left\n    54→\n    55→## Important Notes\n    56→- GH_TOKEN loaded from `.claude-beat/.env`\n    57→- Remote URL includes PAT: reset with `git remote set-url` if needed\n    58→- Next.js 15 used (not 16) due to Turbopack /root permission issue\n    59→- agent-browser WORKS if: export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"\n    60→- agent-browser crashes after extended use (memory pressure) — keep recording sessions short, restart between scenes\n    61→- ffmpeg installed as static binary in ~/bin — add to PATH: export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"\n    62→- ELEVENLABS_API_KEY not available — demo video has no voiceover\n    63→- Demo video at .claude-beat/logs/demos/demo_2026-03-20.mp4 (5 scenes, ~1:51)\n    64→- SQLite DB stored in `app/data/` (gitignored)\n    65→- DB tables: users, listings, listing_members, listing_applications, user_genres, notifications, ratings, telegram_chats, pending_telegram_groups\n    66→- Telegram bot integration requires TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN env var (see issue #21)\n    67→- Discord bot integration requires DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN + DISCORD_CLIENT_ID + DISCORD_WEBHOOK_SECRET env vars\n    68→- Listings have `platform_preference` field (telegram/discord)\n    69→- Email notifications require SMTP env vars (SMTP_HOST, SMTP_PORT, SMTP_FROM, etc.)\n    70→- nodemailer added as dependency for email sending\n    71→- Session secret: lazy runtime check (not module-level) to avoid build failure\n    72→- ESLint: use eslint v9 + @eslint/js v9 (not v10) to avoid peer dep conflicts\n    73→- npm ci works cleanly (no --legacy-peer-deps needed)\n    74→- No root/sudo access on server — can't install nginx, Docker, or system packages\n    75→- PM2 available via npx (not globally installed)\n    76→\n    77→## Test Suite\n    78→- 59 tests across 8 test files, all passing\n    79→- Tested: auth (login, register), health, listings, notifications, profile, ratings, rate-limit\n    80→- Not yet tested: telegram, discord, profile/genres, profile/reading, profile/reputation, listing detail routes\n    81→- Coverage: ~88% statement coverage on tested files\n    82→- @vitest/coverage-v8 installed for coverage reporting\n    83→\n    84→## Next Session Priority\n    85→1. **URGENT**: PAT expires ~March 27 (#54) — ~6 days remaining, still needs-human\n    86→2. Check issue #21 for Telegram token response\n    87→3. Check production health\n    88→4. Consider proposing more improvements (test coverage for remaining routes, E2E tests, etc.)\n    89→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"6f88fa39-6af5-4e21-af20-53fcaa16208c","uuid":"e1d4567e-f701-4a41-9c52-793a70af5675","timestamp":"2026-03-23T13:00:19.785Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/memory/MEMORY.md","content":"# Agent Memory\n\n## Project\n- **Name**: Bookmate\n- **Repo**: https://github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate\n- **Project Board**: Bookmate (project #1 on TommyBrennan)\n- **Tech**: Next.js 15, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4, SQLite, iron-session\n- **Owner**: XanderZhu (human), TommyBrennan (GH account)\n\n## Current State\n- All PRD features implemented and merged (P0, P1, P2)\n- CI pipeline live: GitHub Actions runs lint + type check + build on push/PR to main\n- ESLint flat config with typescript-eslint, react-hooks, @next/eslint-plugin-next\n- Build passes clean, lint clean (no warnings)\n- Security hardening merged (PR #32, #36)\n- **Production deployed** via PM2 on port 3000 — http://89.167.127.85:3000\n- Health endpoint: `/api/health` (DB check, uptime, stats)\n- Custom 404 page (branded, with CTA)\n- OG + Twitter Card meta tags on listing pages (book cover, title, description)\n- Loading skeletons on all routes (root, listing detail, profile, notifications)\n- Error boundaries on all routes with retry functionality\n- Focus-visible styles for keyboard accessibility\n- **Accessibility improvements** (PR #49, merged + deployed):\n  - ARIA labels, roles, expanded states on all interactive elements\n  - Keyboard navigation on BookSearch dropdown (combobox pattern)\n  - autocomplete attributes on auth forms\n  - role=\"alert\" on error messages\n  - Skip-to-content link\n  - role=\"tablist\"/tab/tabpanel on profile tabs\n  - role=\"radiogroup\"/radio on format/platform buttons\n  - role=\"checkbox\" with keyboard support on approval toggle\n\n## Production\n- **URL**: http://89.167.127.85:3000\n- **Process manager**: PM2 (via npx)\n- **Config**: `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n- **Deploy script**: `scripts/deploy.sh`\n- **Logs**: `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n- **No reverse proxy** (nginx requires root — not available)\n- **No HTTPS** (no domain configured, no certbot)\n- PM2 process saved: `npx pm2 save` (restores on `pm2 resurrect`)\n\n## Open PRs\n- None\n\n## Closed Recently\n- #60: Expanded test coverage to 59 tests (notifications, profile, ratings) — merged\n- #59: Vitest test infrastructure with 28 initial tests — merged\n- #58: next/image optimization + SEO (robots.txt, sitemap.xml) — merged + deployed\n\n## Open Issues\n- #21: Telegram bot token (needs-human) — multiple follow-up reminders sent, still waiting\n- #54: **PAT expires ~March 27** (needs-human) — GH_TOKEN renewal required, ~6 days left\n\n## Important Notes\n- GH_TOKEN loaded from `.claude-beat/.env`\n- Remote URL includes PAT: reset with `git remote set-url` if needed\n- Next.js 15 used (not 16) due to Turbopack /root permission issue\n- agent-browser WORKS if: export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"\n- agent-browser crashes after extended use (memory pressure) — keep recording sessions short, restart between scenes\n- ffmpeg installed as static binary in ~/bin — add to PATH: export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"\n- ELEVENLABS_API_KEY not available — demo video has no voiceover\n- Demo video at .claude-beat/logs/demos/demo_2026-03-20.mp4 (5 scenes, ~1:51)\n- SQLite DB stored in `app/data/` (gitignored)\n- DB tables: users, listings, listing_members, listing_applications, user_genres, notifications, ratings, telegram_chats, pending_telegram_groups\n- Telegram bot integration requires TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN env var (see issue #21)\n- Discord bot integration requires DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN + DISCORD_CLIENT_ID + DISCORD_WEBHOOK_SECRET env vars\n- Listings have `platform_preference` field (telegram/discord)\n- Email notifications require SMTP env vars (SMTP_HOST, SMTP_PORT, SMTP_FROM, etc.)\n- nodemailer added as dependency for email sending\n- Session secret: lazy runtime check (not module-level) to avoid build failure\n- ESLint: use eslint v9 + @eslint/js v9 (not v10) to avoid peer dep conflicts\n- npm ci works cleanly (no --legacy-peer-deps needed)\n- No root/sudo access on server — can't install nginx, Docker, or system packages\n- PM2 available via npx (not globally installed)\n\n## Test Suite\n- 59 tests across 8 test files, all passing\n- Tested: auth (login, register), health, listings, notifications, profile, ratings, rate-limit\n- Not yet tested: telegram, discord, profile/genres, profile/reading, profile/reputation, listing detail routes\n- Coverage: ~88% statement coverage on tested files\n- @vitest/coverage-v8 installed for coverage reporting\n\n## Next Session Priority\n1. **URGENT**: PAT expires ~March 27 (#54) — ~6 days remaining, still needs-human\n2. Check issue #21 for Telegram token response\n3. Check production health\n4. Consider proposing more improvements (test coverage for remaining routes, E2E tests, etc.)\n","numLines":89,"startLine":1,"totalLines":89}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01WwBJJ1wB6V5txoH7kg8BXA","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Learned Patterns\n     2→\n     3→## GitHub Authentication\n     4→- Always export GH_TOKEN from .env before running gh commands:\n     5→  ```bash\n     6→  export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2)\n     7→  ```\n     8→- Cannot `--add-reviewer` when token owner is the PR author (HTTP 422)\n     9→- Repo created under TommyBrennan (not XanderZhu — can't create for other users)\n    10→\n    11→## Session Flow\n    12→- First session bootstraps repo, creates issues from PRD as `approved` + `prd`\n    13→- PRD features are pre-approved — no proposal step needed\n    14→- Focus on housekeeping when blocked: organize project board, update memory\n    15→\n    16→## Environment\n    17→- Find agent-browser with: `which agent-browser`\n    18→- agent-browser requires `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp` prefix due to socket dir permissions\n    19→- Playwright chromium needs manual install: `npx playwright install chromium`\n    20→- Git remote uses PAT in URL: `https://ghp_...@github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate.git`\n    21→- Git remote URL may lose PAT between sessions — re-set with `git remote set-url`\n    22→- Must set `git config user.email` and `user.name` each session if not set globally\n    23→\n    24→## Build\n    25→- Next.js 16 Turbopack fails in this environment (can't read /root dir)\n    26→- Use Next.js 15 which defaults to webpack\n    27→- Build from app/ directory: `cd app && npx next build`\n    28→- `useRef<T>()` needs explicit `undefined` arg in React 19: `useRef<T>(undefined)`\n    29→- better-sqlite3 `.all()` returns `unknown[]` — cast explicitly for TS\n    30→\n    31→## Testing\n    32→- Vitest is the test runner: `cd app && npm test`\n    33→- Tests live in `app/__tests__/` with helpers in `app/__tests__/helpers/`\n    34→- Use `vi.hoisted()` with inline `require(\"better-sqlite3\")` to create in-memory DBs for vi.mock factories\n    35→- vi.mock is hoisted above imports — can't reference variables declared after it; use `vi.hoisted()` for shared state\n    36→- Mock bcryptjs to avoid slow hashing (cost factor 12) in unit tests\n    37→- Mock `@/lib/session` to avoid `next/headers` dependency outside request context\n    38→- Route uses `q` param for search (not `search`) and `meeting_format` for format filter (not `format`)\n    39→- ESLint allows `require()` in test files (needed for vi.hoisted pattern)\n    40→- `RequestInit` type needs cast to `NextRequest` for NextRequest constructor compatibility\n    41→\n    42→## Browser Testing\n    43→- agent-browser WORKS with: `export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"`\n    44→- Crashes after extended use (~5+ min) due to Chromium memory pressure\n    45→- Restart between recording scenes: `agent-browser close && rm -rf $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/agent-browser/ && sleep 2`\n    46→- For snapshot timeouts on pages with many elements (e.g., book search dropdown), use `agent-browser screenshot` or JS eval instead\n    47→- Book search results can't use `find text` (multiple matches) — use JS: `document.querySelector(\"button[type=button].w-full\").click()`\n    48→- Reading pace input ref (@e17) times out — use `agent-browser eval` with nativeInputValueSetter to set values\n    49→- For API testing: `curl -s URL | python3 -m json.tool`\n    50→- ffmpeg: static binary at ~/bin/ffmpeg — `export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"` needed each session\n    51→- When recording demos: login user BEFORE starting recording, then navigate to target page\n    52→\n    53→## Deployment\n    54→- No root/sudo access — can't apt-get install, create systemd services, or install nginx\n    55→- PM2 available via npx (not global): `npx pm2 start/stop/restart/list/logs`\n    56→- Deploy script: `./scripts/deploy.sh` (pull, install, build, restart PM2)\n    57→- Production runs on port 3000 directly (no reverse proxy)\n    58→- Public IP: 89.167.127.85 (IPv4), 2a01:4f9:c013:600f::1 (IPv6)\n    59→- Kill stale dev servers before deploy: check `ss -tlnp | grep 3000`\n    60→- PM2 ecosystem config at `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n    61→- PM2 logs at `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n    62→\n    63→## React + SQLite\n    64→- SQLite stores booleans as integers (0/1). In JSX, `{0 && <Component />}` renders \"0\" as text. Always use `!!value` or `Boolean(value)` for numeric fields in `&&` conditionals.\n    65→- Git `add` with bracket directories like `[id]` needs escaping or use from project root\n    66→\n    67→## Security\n    68→- Telegram webhook commands (/link, /link_ID) must verify the sender is the listing author via telegram_user_links table\n    69→- Always validate external URLs before storing (e.g., book_cover_url must start with https://covers.openlibrary.org/)\n    70→- Notification failures should never mask successful DB transactions — wrap in separate try/catch\n    71→- Move related DB writes inside the same transaction for atomicity (e.g., rejection of pending apps when group fills)\n    72→- Query string parameters from URL are always strings — parseInt() before DB queries on integer columns\n    73→\n    74→## Working Directory\n    75→- Project root: /root/Projects/bookmate\n    76→- App directory: /root/Projects/bookmate/app\n    77→- Always use full paths for .claude-beat/.env references\n    78→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"6f88fa39-6af5-4e21-af20-53fcaa16208c","uuid":"6f06fe41-458a-4cc7-ae25-84947ad377b5","timestamp":"2026-03-23T13:00:20.258Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/memory/patterns.md","content":"# Learned Patterns\n\n## GitHub Authentication\n- Always export GH_TOKEN from .env before running gh commands:\n  ```bash\n  export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2)\n  ```\n- Cannot `--add-reviewer` when token owner is the PR author (HTTP 422)\n- Repo created under TommyBrennan (not XanderZhu — can't create for other users)\n\n## Session Flow\n- First session bootstraps repo, creates issues from PRD as `approved` + `prd`\n- PRD features are pre-approved — no proposal step needed\n- Focus on housekeeping when blocked: organize project board, update memory\n\n## Environment\n- Find agent-browser with: `which agent-browser`\n- agent-browser requires `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp` prefix due to socket dir permissions\n- Playwright chromium needs manual install: `npx playwright install chromium`\n- Git remote uses PAT in URL: `https://ghp_...@github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate.git`\n- Git remote URL may lose PAT between sessions — re-set with `git remote set-url`\n- Must set `git config user.email` and `user.name` each session if not set globally\n\n## Build\n- Next.js 16 Turbopack fails in this environment (can't read /root dir)\n- Use Next.js 15 which defaults to webpack\n- Build from app/ directory: `cd app && npx next build`\n- `useRef<T>()` needs explicit `undefined` arg in React 19: `useRef<T>(undefined)`\n- better-sqlite3 `.all()` returns `unknown[]` — cast explicitly for TS\n\n## Testing\n- Vitest is the test runner: `cd app && npm test`\n- Tests live in `app/__tests__/` with helpers in `app/__tests__/helpers/`\n- Use `vi.hoisted()` with inline `require(\"better-sqlite3\")` to create in-memory DBs for vi.mock factories\n- vi.mock is hoisted above imports — can't reference variables declared after it; use `vi.hoisted()` for shared state\n- Mock bcryptjs to avoid slow hashing (cost factor 12) in unit tests\n- Mock `@/lib/session` to avoid `next/headers` dependency outside request context\n- Route uses `q` param for search (not `search`) and `meeting_format` for format filter (not `format`)\n- ESLint allows `require()` in test files (needed for vi.hoisted pattern)\n- `RequestInit` type needs cast to `NextRequest` for NextRequest constructor compatibility\n\n## Browser Testing\n- agent-browser WORKS with: `export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"`\n- Crashes after extended use (~5+ min) due to Chromium memory pressure\n- Restart between recording scenes: `agent-browser close && rm -rf $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/agent-browser/ && sleep 2`\n- For snapshot timeouts on pages with many elements (e.g., book search dropdown), use `agent-browser screenshot` or JS eval instead\n- Book search results can't use `find text` (multiple matches) — use JS: `document.querySelector(\"button[type=button].w-full\").click()`\n- Reading pace input ref (@e17) times out — use `agent-browser eval` with nativeInputValueSetter to set values\n- For API testing: `curl -s URL | python3 -m json.tool`\n- ffmpeg: static binary at ~/bin/ffmpeg — `export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"` needed each session\n- When recording demos: login user BEFORE starting recording, then navigate to target page\n\n## Deployment\n- No root/sudo access — can't apt-get install, create systemd services, or install nginx\n- PM2 available via npx (not global): `npx pm2 start/stop/restart/list/logs`\n- Deploy script: `./scripts/deploy.sh` (pull, install, build, restart PM2)\n- Production runs on port 3000 directly (no reverse proxy)\n- Public IP: 89.167.127.85 (IPv4), 2a01:4f9:c013:600f::1 (IPv6)\n- Kill stale dev servers before deploy: check `ss -tlnp | grep 3000`\n- PM2 ecosystem config at `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n- PM2 logs at `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n\n## React + SQLite\n- SQLite stores booleans as integers (0/1). In JSX, `{0 && <Component />}` renders \"0\" as text. Always use `!!value` or `Boolean(value)` for numeric fields in `&&` conditionals.\n- Git `add` with bracket directories like `[id]` needs escaping or use from project root\n\n## Security\n- Telegram webhook commands (/link, /link_ID) must verify the sender is the listing author via telegram_user_links table\n- Always validate external URLs before storing (e.g., book_cover_url must start with https://covers.openlibrary.org/)\n- Notification failures should never mask successful DB transactions — wrap in separate try/catch\n- Move related DB writes inside the same transaction for atomicity (e.g., rejection of pending apps when group fills)\n- Query string parameters from URL are always strings — parseInt() before DB queries on integer columns\n\n## Working Directory\n- Project root: /root/Projects/bookmate\n- App directory: /root/Projects/bookmate/app\n- Always use full paths for .claude-beat/.env references\n","numLines":78,"startLine":1,"totalLines":78}}}

   24→{\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Base directory for this skill: /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude/skills/session-workflow\\n\\n# Session Workflow\\n\\nThis skill expands each step of the core loop: **READ → REVIEW → DECIDE → ACT → VERIFY → DOCUMENT**.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 1. READ — Load Context\\n\\n- Check if `REPOSITORY.md` exists. If not, invoke the `repo-bootstrap` skill before proceeding.\\n- Read `.claude-beat/PRD.md`, `.claude-beat/memory/MEMORY.md`, `.claude-beat/memory/patterns.md`\\n- If PRD.md references additional files, read those too\\n- Read the last 2–3 session logs from `.claude-beat/logs/sessions/`\\n- Fetch open issues and PRs via `gh` CLI\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 2. REVIEW — Assess Current State\\n\\nBefore picking new work, check what needs attention right now.\\n\\n### 2a. Production Health Check (every session)\\n\\nIf the project has a production or staging environment:\\n\\n1. Identify the production URL (from memory, `.env`, deploy config, or README). If no production URL can be found, skip the production health check and note \\\"No production environment detected\\\" in the session log.\\n2. Use `which agent-browser` to locate the browser tool. Always attempt browser verification before falling back to non-visual checks. Open the production URL and verify core functionality works.\\n3. Check for visible errors, broken pages, or degraded behavior\\n4. If the platform provides logs or monitoring (Render, Vercel, etc.) — check recent error logs\\n\\n**If production issues are found:**\\n- **Critical** (app down, core flow broken, data loss risk): fix immediately in this session, create a PR, deploy\\n- **Medium** (non-blocking bugs, UI glitches, non-critical errors): create a GitHub issue labeled `approved`, `bug` with priority `P1`\\n- **Low** (cosmetic, minor warnings): create a GitHub issue labeled `approved`, `bug` with priority `P2`\\n\\n### 2b. Follow Up on Previous Sessions\\n\\nRead the most recent session logs and check for:\\n\\n- **Deployments**: if something was deployed last session, verify it is live and working (part of 2a)\\n- **Human action requests**: if a previous session created a `needs-human` or `blocked` issue, check whether the human has acted on it\\n  - If resolved — acknowledge, close the issue, continue with any unblocked work\\n  - If stale (no response for 2+ sessions) — add a comment on the issue reminding the owner, and note it in the session log\\n- **Open PRs with review comments**: if a PR has unaddressed reviewer feedback, respond and update before opening new PRs\\n- **WIP branches**: if the previous session left work-in-progress, pick it up and finish it\\n\\n### 2c. PRD Sync\\n\\nScan PRD (and any referenced spec files) for features with no corresponding GitHub issue. For each missing feature:\\n\\n- Label: `approved` + `prd` + priority (`P0`/`P1`/`P2` matching the PRD)\\n- Title prefix: `prd:` (e.g., `prd: add CEFR level selector`)\\n- These are pre-approved — no proposal step, no waiting for human reaction\\n\\nSearch existing issues first to avoid duplicates (`gh issue list --state all`). This step runs every session regardless of other work.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 3. DECIDE — Pick What to Work On\\n\\nWrite a brief triage summary in your session log before starting work:\\n- Production status (healthy / issues found)\\n- Follow-ups resolved or escalated\\n- What you will work on this session and why\\n\\nSelect work in this priority order:\\n\\n1. **Critical production issues** found during REVIEW\\n2. **Approved `P0` issues** — launch blockers, critical bugs, critical PRD features\\n3. **Open PRs needing attention** — review comments, merge-ready PRs, CI failures\\n4. **In-progress work** from previous sessions (WIP branches, draft PRs)\\n5. **Approved `P1` and `P2` issues** — P1 first, then P2\\n6. **Stale proposals** — if a `proposal` issue has had no human reaction for 2+ sessions, change label to `approved` and comment \\\"Auto-approving after no objection.\\\" Never self-approve issues labeled `needs-human` or `blocked`.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 4. ACT — Execute\\n\\nFor each piece of work:\\n1. Implement the change\\n2. Run lint, tests, build\\n3. Test using `agent-browser` skill if UI-related\\n4. Commit and create/update PR (use `pr-lifecycle` skill)\\n5. If the primary item finishes quickly and there is a small, self-contained second item, you may take it on. Do not start a third item — leave it for the next session.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 5. VERIFY — Post-Deploy Check\\n\\nIf you deployed anything this session:\\n1. Verify the deployment succeeded\\n2. If browser testing tooling is available, open production and confirm the change is live\\n3. Check for regressions — test core flows, not just the new feature\\n\\nIf you opened or updated a PR this session:\\n- Verify CI checks are passing before closing the session\\n\\nIf you did NOT deploy but production had issues in REVIEW:\\n- Confirm your fix PR is open and ready for review\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 6. DOCUMENT — Close the Session\\n\\n1. Write session log to `.claude-beat/logs/sessions/YYYY-MM-DD_HH-MM.md`\\n2. Update `.claude-beat/memory/MEMORY.md`\\n3. Update `.claude-beat/memory/patterns.md` if you learned something new\\n4. Ensure no uncommitted changes remain (`git status`)\\n5. All branches pushed, all work has a PR (draft is fine for WIP)\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Quick Reference: Session Checklist\\n\\n```\\nREAD\\n[ ] Context loaded (PRD, memory, logs, issues, PRs)\\n\\nREVIEW\\n[ ] Production health checked\\n[ ] Previous session follow-ups addressed\\n\\nDECIDE\\n[ ] Triage summary written\\n[ ] Work selected by priority\\n\\nACT\\n[ ] Changes implemented, tested, PR opened\\n\\nVERIFY\\n[ ] CI checks passing (if PR opened)\\n[ ] Production check (if deployed)\\n\\nDOCUMENT\\n[ ] Session log written\\n[ ] Memory updated\\n[ ] No uncommitted changes\\n```\\n\"}]},\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"session_id\":\"6f88fa39-6af5-4e21-af20-53fcaa16208c\",\"uuid\":\"9d547629-29da-4e73-bb5c-bbb50c7eee2c\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-03-23T13:00:09.952Z\",\"isSynthetic\":true}\n session-workflow\"}]},\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"session_id\":\"6f88fa39-6af5-4e21-af20-53fcaa16208c\",\"uuid\":\"4b36aecb-38db-47c4-b780-14a2df037898\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-03-23T13:00:09.953Z\",\"tool_use_result\":{\"success\":true,\"commandName\":\"session-workflow\"}}\n{\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Base directory for this skill: /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude/skills/session-workflow\\n\\n# Session Workflow\\n\\nThis skill expands each step of the core loop: **READ → REVIEW → DECIDE → ACT → VERIFY → DOCUMENT**.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 1. READ — Load Context\\n\\n- Check if `REPOSITORY.md` exists. If not, invoke the `repo-bootstrap` skill before proceeding.\\n- Read `.claude-beat/PRD.md`, `.claude-beat/memory/MEMORY.md`, `.claude-beat/memory/patterns.md`\\n- If PRD.md references additional files, read those too\\n- Read the last 2–3 session logs from `.claude-beat/logs/sessions/`\\n- Fetch open issues and PRs via `gh` CLI\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 2. REVIEW — Assess Current State\\n\\nBefore picking new work, check what needs attention right now.\\n\\n### 2a. Production Health Check (every session)\\n\\nIf the project has a production or staging environment:\\n\\n1. Identify the production URL (from memory, `.env`, deploy config, or README). If no production URL can be found, skip the production health check and note \\\"No production environment detected\\\" in the session log.\\n2. Use `which agent-browser` to locate the browser tool. Always attempt browser verification before falling back to non-visual checks. Open the production URL and verify core functionality works.\\n3. Check for visible errors, broken pages, or degraded behavior\\n4. If the platform provides logs or monitoring (Render, Vercel, etc.) — check recent error logs\\n\\n**If production issues are found:**\\n- **Critical** (app down, core flow broken, data loss risk): fix immediately in this session, create a PR, deploy\\n- **Medium** (non-blocking bugs, UI glitches, non-critical errors): create a GitHub issue labeled `approved`, `bug` with priority `P1`\\n- **Low** (cosmetic, minor warnings): create a GitHub issue labeled `approved`, `bug` with priority `P2`\\n\\n### 2b. Follow Up on Previous Sessions\\n\\nRead the most recent session logs and check for:\\n\\n- **Deployments**: if something was deployed last session, verify it is live and working (part of 2a)\\n- **Human action requests**: if a previous session created a `needs-human` or `blocked` issue, check whether the human has acted on it\\n  - If resolved — acknowledge, close the issue, continue with any unblocked work\\n  - If stale (no response for 2+ sessions) — add a comment on the issue reminding the owner, and note it in the session log\\n- **Open PRs with review comments**: if a PR has unaddressed reviewer feedback, respond and update before opening new PRs\\n- **WIP branches**: if the previous session left work-in-progress, pick it up and finish it\\n\\n### 2c. PRD Sync\\n\\nScan PRD (and any referenced spec files) for features with no corresponding GitHub issue. For each missing feature:\\n\\n- Label: `approved` + `prd` + priority (`P0`/`P1`/`P2` matching the PRD)\\n- Title prefix: `prd:` (e.g., `prd: add CEFR level selector`)\\n- These are pre-approved — no proposal step, no waiting for human reaction\\n\\nSearch existing issues first to avoid duplicates (`gh issue list --state all`). This step runs every session regardless of other work.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 3. DECIDE — Pick What to Work On\\n\\nWrite a brief triage summary in your session log before starting work:\\n- Production status (healthy / issues found)\\n- Follow-ups resolved or escalated\\n- What you will work on this session and why\\n\\nSelect work in this priority order:\\n\\n1. **Critical production issues** found during REVIEW\\n2. **Approved `P0` issues** — launch blockers, critical bugs, critical PRD features\\n3. **Open PRs needing attention** — review comments, merge-ready PRs, CI failures\\n4. **In-progress work** from previous sessions (WIP branches, draft PRs)\\n5. **Approved `P1` and `P2` issues** — P1 first, then P2\\n6. **Stale proposals** — if a `proposal` issue has had no human reaction for 2+ sessions, change label to `approved` and comment \\\"Auto-approving after no objection.\\\" Never self-approve issues labeled `needs-human` or `blocked`.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 4. ACT — Execute\\n\\nFor each piece of work:\\n1. Implement the change\\n2. Run lint, tests, build\\n3. Test using `agent-browser` skill if UI-related\\n4. Commit and create/update PR (use `pr-lifecycle` skill)\\n5. If the primary item finishes quickly and there is a small, self-contained second item, you may take it on. Do not start a third item — leave it for the next session.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 5. VERIFY — Post-Deploy Check\\n\\nIf you deployed anything this session:\\n1. Verify the deployment succeeded\\n2. If browser testing tooling is available, open production and confirm the change is live\\n3. Check for regressions — test core flows, not just the new feature\\n\\nIf you opened or updated a PR this session:\\n- Verify CI checks are passing before closing the session\\n\\nIf you did NOT deploy but production had issues in REVIEW:\\n- Confirm your fix PR is open and ready for review\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 6. DOCUMENT — Close the Session\\n\\n1. Write session log to `.claude-beat/logs/sessions/YYYY-MM-DD_HH-MM.md`\\n2. Update `.claude-beat/memory/MEMORY.md`\\n3. Update `.claude-beat/memory/patterns.md` if you learned something new\\n4. Ensure no uncommitted changes remain (`git status`)\\n5. All branches pushed, all work has a PR (draft is fine for WIP)\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Quick Reference: Session Checklist\\n\\n```\\nREAD\\n[ ] Context loaded (PRD, memory, logs, issues, PRs)\\n\\nREVIEW\\n[ ] Production health checked\\n[ ] Previous session follow-ups addressed\\n\\nDECIDE\\n[ ] Triage summary written\\n[ ] Work selected by priority\\n\\nACT\\n[ ] Changes implemented, tested, PR opened\\n\\nVERIFY\\n[ ] CI checks passing (if PR opened)\\n[ ] Production check (if deployed)\\n\\nDOCUMENT\\n[ ] Session log written\\n[ ] Memory updated\\n[ ] No uncommitted changes\\n```\\n\"}]},\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"session_id\":\"6f88fa39-6af5-4e21-af20-53fcaa16208c\",\"uuid\":\"9d547629-29da-4e73-bb5c-bbb50c7eee2c\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-03-23T13:00:09.952Z\",\"isSynthetic\":true}\n{\"type\":\"stream_event\",\"event\":{\"type\":\"message_stop\"},\"session_id\":\"6f88fa39-6af5-4e21-af20-53fcaa16208c\",\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"uuid\":\"e2c94f72-75c6-45fa-bd4e-0e864f785f54\"}\n{\"type\":\"rate_limit_event\",\"rate_limit_info\":{\"status\":\"allowed\",\"resetsAt\":1774278000,\"rateLimitType\":\"five_hour\",\"overageStatus\":\"rejected\",\"overageDisabledReason\":\"out_of_credits\",\"isUsingOverage\":false},\"uuid\":\"c6a3a7bd-4e3a-47cb-a2a6-79ce40d65fea\",\"session_id\":\"6f88fa39-6af5-4e21-af20-53fcaa16208c\"}\n{\"type\":\"stream_event\",\"event\":{\"type\":\"message_start\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-opus-4-6\",\"id\":\"msg_011vPujbXZnb3LXWfzAC4KyU\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":2,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":1622,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":13765,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":1622},\"output_tokens\":9,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}}},\"session_id\":\"6f88fa39-6af5-4e21-af20-53fcaa16208c\",\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"uuid\":\"dfbf95d5-82a6-41b1-bd82-9ec8b9268b3e\"}\n{\"type\":\"stream_event\",\"event\":{\"type\":\"content_block_start\",\"index\":0,\"content_block\":{\"type\":\"thinking\",\"thinking\":\"\",\"signature\":\"\"}},\"session_id\":\"6f88fa39-6af5-4e21-af20-53fcaa16208c\",\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"uuid\":\"f160309c-f45e-412c-a474-5ff6822fef89\"}\n{\"type\":\"stream_event\",\"event\":{\"type\":\"content_block_delta\",\"index\":0,\"delta\":{\"type\":\"thinking_delta\",\"thinking\":\"Let\"}},\"session_id\":\"6f88fa39-6af5-4e21-af20-53fcaa16208c\",\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"uuid\":\"97975ff5-6d84-4aa2-81de-ac5fcf26 {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01LWn9WxVkNLWrbe3A1jpp14","type":"tool_result","content":"## Problem\nListings with start dates that have already passed still show up in the browse feed. These are effectively stale — the reading group either started without filling or was abandoned.\n\n## Proposed Solution\n1. Filter out listings where `start_date < today` from the main browse API by default\n2. Add a query param `?include_past=true` for viewing past listings if needed\n3. Optionally: add a \"Past\" status badge on the listing detail page for listings with past start dates\n\n## Scope\nSmall change — 1-2 lines in the listings API query + optional UI badge.","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"6f88fa39-6af5-4e21-af20-53fcaa16208c","uuid":"c6c857b8-4fac-43f0-92cd-435c950eb4d0","timestamp":"2026-03-23T13:02:14.586Z","tool_use_result":{"stdout":"## Problem\nListings with start dates that have already passed still show up in the browse feed. These are effectively stale — the reading group either started without filling or was abandoned.\n\n## Proposed Solution\n1. Filter out listings where `start_date < today` from the main browse API by default\n2. Add a query param `?include_past=true` for viewing past listings if needed\n3. Optionally: add a \"Past\" status badge on the listing detail page for listings with past start dates\n\n## Scope\nSmall change — 1-2 lines in the listings API query + optional UI badge.","stderr":"","interrupted":false,"isImage":false,"noOutputExpected":false}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01NanharLuvyVbY9BKTsJZeH","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→\n     4→const PAGE_SIZE = 20;\n     5→\n     6→export async function GET(request: NextRequest) {\n     7→  const { searchParams } = request.nextUrl;\n     8→\n     9→  const q = searchParams.get(\"q\")?.trim() || \"\";\n    10→  const meetingFormat = searchParams.get(\"meeting_format\") || \"\";\n    11→  const readingPace = searchParams.get(\"reading_pace\") || \"\";\n    12→  const startDateFrom = searchParams.get(\"start_date_from\") || \"\";\n    13→  const sort = searchParams.get(\"sort\") || \"newest\";\n    14→  const pageParam = parseInt(searchParams.get(\"page\") || \"1\", 10);\n    15→  const page = isNaN(pageParam) || pageParam < 1 ? 1 : pageParam;\n    16→\n    17→  const conditions: string[] = [\"l.is_full = 0\"];\n    18→  const params: (string | number)[] = [];\n    19→\n    20→  if (q && q.length <= 300) {\n    21→    conditions.push(\"(l.book_title LIKE ? OR l.book_author LIKE ?)\");\n    22→    params.push(`%${q}%`, `%${q}%`);\n    23→  }\n    24→\n    25→  if (meetingFormat && [\"voice\", \"text\", \"mixed\"].includes(meetingFormat)) {\n    26→    conditions.push(\"l.meeting_format = ?\");\n    27→    params.push(meetingFormat);\n    28→  }\n    29→\n    30→  if (readingPace && readingPace.length <= 200) {\n    31→    conditions.push(\"l.reading_pace LIKE ?\");\n    32→    params.push(`%${readingPace}%`);\n    33→  }\n    34→\n    35→  if (startDateFrom && /^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}$/.test(startDateFrom)) {\n    36→    conditions.push(\"l.start_date >= ?\");\n    37→    params.push(startDateFrom);\n    38→  }\n    39→\n    40→  const whereClause = conditions.join(\" AND \");\n    41→\n    42→  let orderClause = \"l.created_at DESC\";\n    43→  if (sort === \"oldest\") orderClause = \"l.created_at ASC\";\n    44→  else if (sort === \"start_date\") orderClause = \"l.start_date ASC\";\n    45→\n    46→  const offset = (page - 1) * PAGE_SIZE;\n    47→\n    48→  // Get total count for pagination metadata\n    49→  const countResult = db\n    50→    .prepare(\n    51→      `SELECT COUNT(*) as total FROM listings l WHERE ${whereClause}`\n    52→    )\n    53→    .get(...params) as { total: number };\n    54→\n    55→  const listings = db\n    56→    .prepare(\n    57→      `SELECT\n    58→        l.*,\n    59→        u.display_name as author_name,\n    60→        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = l.id) as member_count\n    61→      FROM listings l\n    62→      JOIN users u ON l.author_id = u.id\n    63→      WHERE ${whereClause}\n    64→      ORDER BY ${orderClause}\n    65→      LIMIT ? OFFSET ?`\n    66→    )\n    67→    .all(...params, PAGE_SIZE, offset);\n    68→\n    69→  return NextResponse.json({\n    70→    listings,\n    71→    pagination: {\n    72→      page,\n    73→      pageSize: PAGE_SIZE,\n    74→      total: countResult.total,\n    75→      totalPages: Math.ceil(countResult.total / PAGE_SIZE),\n    76→    },\n    77→  });\n    78→}\n    79→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_017Hd1VhCdyVw9bYdQVD7FVR","session_id":"6f88fa39-6af5-4e21-af20-53fcaa16208c","uuid":"555ee46b-ecea-4682-8564-7239b3ab23fe","timestamp":"2026-03-23T13:02:23.463Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_019BJiW8PinVNWvCF3WSh5HJ","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import type { Metadata } from \"next\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import ListingDetail from \"./ListingDetail\";\n     4→\n     5→interface ListingRow {\n     6→  book_title: string;\n     7→  book_author: string;\n     8→  book_cover_url: string;\n     9→  reading_pace: string;\n    10→  author_name: string;\n    11→  max_group_size: number;\n    12→}\n    13→\n    14→export async function generateMetadata({\n    15→  params,\n    16→}: {\n    17→  params: Promise<{ id: string }>;\n    18→}): Promise<Metadata> {\n    19→  const { id } = await params;\n    20→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    21→\n    22→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    23→    return { title: \"Listing not found — Bookmate\" };\n    24→  }\n    25→\n    26→  try {\n    27→    const listing = db\n    28→      .prepare(\n    29→        `SELECT l.book_title, l.book_author, l.book_cover_url, l.reading_pace,\n    30→                l.max_group_size, u.display_name as author_name\n    31→         FROM listings l\n    32→         JOIN users u ON l.author_id = u.id\n    33→         WHERE l.id = ?`\n    34→      )\n    35→      .get(listingId) as ListingRow | undefined;\n    36→\n    37→    if (!listing) {\n    38→      return { title: \"Listing not found — Bookmate\" };\n    39→    }\n    40→\n    41→    const title = `${listing.book_title} by ${listing.book_author} — Bookmate`;\n    42→    const description = `Join a reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" by ${listing.book_author}. Pace: ${listing.reading_pace}. Up to ${listing.max_group_size} readers. Posted by ${listing.author_name}.`;\n    43→\n    44→    return {\n    45→      title,\n    46→      description,\n    47→      openGraph: {\n    48→        title,\n    49→        description,\n    50→        type: \"article\",\n    51→        ...(listing.book_cover_url\n    52→          ? {\n    53→              images: [\n    54→                {\n    55→                  url: listing.book_cover_url,\n    56→                  alt: `Cover of ${listing.book_title}`,\n    57→                },\n    58→              ],\n    59→            }\n    60→          : {}),\n    61→      },\n    62→      twitter: {\n    63→        card: listing.book_cover_url ? \"summary_large_image\" : \"summary\",\n    64→        title,\n    65→        description,\n    66→        ...(listing.book_cover_url ? { images: [listing.book_cover_url] } : {}),\n    67→      },\n    68→    };\n    69→  } catch {\n    70→    return { title: \"Listing — Bookmate\" };\n    71→  }\n    72→}\n    73→\n    74→export default function ListingPage() {\n    75→  return <ListingDetail />;\n    76→}\n    77→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_017Hd1VhCdyVw9bYdQVD7FVR","session_id":"6f88fa39-6af5-4e21-af20-53fcaa16208c","uuid":"75798a90-e17f-4b0d-b969-3f819515e4ad","timestamp":"2026-03-23T13:02:23.668Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01GRwPLvpsryMBg1CAEAXn2M","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { getSession, requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { createNotification } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n     5→import { isBotConfigured } from \"@/lib/telegram\";\n     6→import { isBotConfigured as isDiscordBotConfigured } from \"@/lib/discord\";\n     7→\n     8→export async function GET(\n     9→  _req: NextRequest,\n    10→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n    11→) {\n    12→  const { id } = await params;\n    13→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    14→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    15→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n    16→  }\n    17→\n    18→  const listing = db\n    19→    .prepare(\n    20→      `SELECT\n    21→        l.*,\n    22→        u.display_name as author_name\n    23→      FROM listings l\n    24→      JOIN users u ON l.author_id = u.id\n    25→      WHERE l.id = ?`\n    26→    )\n    27→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    28→\n    29→  if (!listing) {\n    30→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    31→  }\n    32→\n    33→  const members = db\n    34→    .prepare(\n    35→      `SELECT u.id, u.display_name, u.bio, lm.joined_at\n    36→       FROM listing_members lm\n    37→       JOIN users u ON lm.user_id = u.id\n    38→       WHERE lm.listing_id = ?\n    39→       ORDER BY lm.joined_at ASC`\n    40→    )\n    41→    .all(listingId) as { id: number; display_name: string; bio: string; joined_at: string }[];\n    42→\n    43→  const session = await getSession();\n    44→  const isMember = session.userId\n    45→    ? members.some((m) => m.id === session.userId)\n    46→    : false;\n    47→  const isAuthor = session.userId\n    48→    ? listing.author_id === session.userId\n    49→    : false;\n    50→\n    51→  // PRD: full listings should be invisible to non-participants\n    52→  if (listing.is_full && !isMember && !isAuthor) {\n    53→    return NextResponse.json(\n    54→      { error: \"This listing is no longer available\" },\n    55→      { status: 404 }\n    56→    );\n    57→  }\n    58→\n    59→  // Check if the current user has a pending application\n    60→  let hasApplied = false;\n    61→  let applicationStatus = \"\";\n    62→  if (session.userId && !isMember && listing.requires_approval) {\n    63→    const app = db\n    64→      .prepare(\n    65→        \"SELECT status FROM listing_applications WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ?\"\n    66→      )\n    67→      .get(listingId, session.userId) as { status: string } | undefined;\n    68→    if (app) {\n    69→      hasApplied = app.status === \"pending\";\n    70→      applicationStatus = app.status;\n    71→    }\n    72→  }\n    73→\n    74→  // Fetch pending applicants if the viewer is the author\n    75→  let pendingApplicants: { application_id: number; id: number; display_name: string; bio: string; applied_at: string }[] = [];\n    76→  if (isAuthor && listing.requires_approval) {\n    77→    pendingApplicants = db\n    78→      .prepare(\n    79→        `SELECT la.id as application_id, u.id, u.display_name, u.bio, la.applied_at\n    80→         FROM listing_applications la\n    81→         JOIN users u ON la.user_id = u.id\n    82→         WHERE la.listing_id = ? AND la.status = 'pending'\n    83→         ORDER BY la.applied_at ASC`\n    84→      )\n    85→      .all(listingId) as { application_id: number; id: number; display_name: string; bio: string; applied_at: string }[];\n    86→  }\n    87→\n    88→  return NextResponse.json({\n    89→    listing: {\n    90→      ...listing,\n    91→      members,\n    92→      memberCount: members.length,\n    93→      isMember,\n    94→      isAuthor,\n    95→      hasApplied,\n    96→      applicationStatus,\n    97→      pendingApplicants,\n    98→      currentUserId: session.userId || null,\n    99→      telegramBotConfigured: isBotConfigured(),\n   100→      discordBotConfigured: isDiscordBotConfigured(),\n   101→    },\n   102→  });\n   103→}\n   104→\n   105→export async function PATCH(\n   106→  req: NextRequest,\n   107→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n   108→) {\n   109→  const session = await requireAuth();\n   110→  if (!session) {\n   111→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n   112→  }\n   113→\n   114→  const { id } = await params;\n   115→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n   116→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n   117→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n   118→  }\n   119→\n   120→  const listing = db\n   121→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n   122→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n   123→\n   124→  if (!listing) {\n   125→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n   126→  }\n   127→\n   128→  if (listing.author_id !== session.userId) {\n   129→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Only the author can edit this listing\" }, { status: 403 });\n   130→  }\n   131→\n   132→  // Cannot edit if a platform link has been shared (group already formed)\n   133→  if (listing.telegram_link || listing.discord_link) {\n   134→    return NextResponse.json(\n   135→      { error: \"Cannot edit a listing after the group chat link has been shared\" },\n   136→      { status: 400 }\n   137→    );\n   138→  }\n   139→\n   140→  try {\n   141→    const body = await req.json();\n   142→    const {\n   143→      language,\n   144→      readingPace,\n   145→      startDate,\n   146→      meetingFormat,\n   147→      maxGroupSize,\n   148→      requiresApproval,\n   149→      platformPreference,\n   150→    } = body;\n   151→\n   152→    // Validate fields that are provided\n   153→    if (readingPace !== undefined && (!readingPace || readingPace.length > 200)) {\n   154→      return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Reading pace is required (max 200 characters)\" }, { status: 400 });\n   155→    }\n   156→\n   157→    if (startDate !== undefined && !/^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}$/.test(startDate)) {\n   158→      return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid start date format (expected YYYY-MM-DD)\" }, { status: 400 });\n   159→    }\n   160→\n   161→    if (meetingFormat !== undefined && ![\"voice\", \"text\", \"mixed\"].includes(meetingFormat)) {\n   162→      return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid meeting format\" }, { status: 400 });\n   163→    }\n   164→\n   165→    if (maxGroupSize !== undefined) {\n   166→      const size = parseInt(maxGroupSize, 10);\n   167→      if (isNaN(size) || size < 2 || size > 20) {\n   168→        return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Group size must be between 2 and 20\" }, { status: 400 });\n   169→      }\n   170→\n   171→      // Cannot reduce below current member count\n   172→      const memberCount = (db\n   173→        .prepare(\"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n   174→        .get(listingId) as { count: number }).count;\n   175→\n   176→      if (size < memberCount) {\n   177→        return NextResponse.json(\n   178→          { error: `Cannot reduce group size below current member count (${memberCount})` },\n   179→          { status: 400 }\n   180→        );\n   181→      }\n   182→    }\n   183→\n   184→    if (platformPreference !== undefined && ![\"telegram\", \"discord\"].includes(platformPreference)) {\n   185→      return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid platform preference\" }, { status: 400 });\n   186→    }\n   187→\n   188→    // Build dynamic UPDATE\n   189→    const updates: string[] = [];\n   190→    const values: unknown[] = [];\n   191→\n   192→    if (language !== undefined) {\n   193→      updates.push(\"language = ?\");\n   194→      values.push(language);\n   195→    }\n   196→    if (readingPace !== undefined) {\n   197→      updates.push(\"reading_pace = ?\");\n   198→      values.push(readingPace.trim());\n   199→    }\n   200→    if (startDate !== undefined) {\n   201→      updates.push(\"start_date = ?\");\n   202→      values.push(startDate);\n   203→    }\n   204→    if (meetingFormat !== undefined) {\n   205→      updates.push(\"meeting_format = ?\");\n   206→      values.push(meetingFormat);\n   207→    }\n   208→    if (maxGroupSize !== undefined) {\n   209→      updates.push(\"max_group_size = ?\");\n   210→      values.push(parseInt(maxGroupSize, 10));\n   211→    }\n   212→    if (requiresApproval !== undefined) {\n   213→      updates.push(\"requires_approval = ?\");\n   214→      values.push(requiresApproval ? 1 : 0);\n   215→    }\n   216→    if (platformPreference !== undefined) {\n   217→      updates.push(\"platform_preference = ?\");\n   218→      values.push(platformPreference);\n   219→    }\n   220→\n   221→    if (updates.length === 0) {\n   222→      return NextResponse.json({ error: \"No fields to update\" }, { status: 400 });\n   223→    }\n   224→\n   225→    values.push(listingId);\n   226→    db.prepare(`UPDATE listings SET ${updates.join(\", \")} WHERE id = ?`).run(...values);\n   227→\n   228→    return NextResponse.json({ success: true });\n   229→  } catch (error) {\n   230→    console.error(\"Update listing error:\", error);\n   231→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Internal server error\" }, { status: 500 });\n   232→  }\n   233→}\n   234→\n   235→export async function DELETE(\n   236→  _req: NextRequest,\n   237→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n   238→) {\n   239→  const session = await requireAuth();\n   240→  if (!session) {\n   241→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n   242→  }\n   243→\n   244→  const { id } = await params;\n   245→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n   246→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n   247→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n   248→  }\n   249→\n   250→  const listing = db\n   251→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n   252→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n   253→\n   254→  if (!listing) {\n   255→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n   256→  }\n   257→\n   258→  if (listing.author_id !== session.userId) {\n   259→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Only the author can delete this listing\" }, { status: 403 });\n   260→  }\n   261→\n   262→  // Cannot delete if a platform link has been shared\n   263→  if (listing.telegram_link || listing.discord_link) {\n   264→    return NextResponse.json(\n   265→      { error: \"Cannot delete a listing after the group chat link has been shared\" },\n   266→      { status: 400 }\n   267→    );\n   268→  }\n   269→\n   270→  try {\n   271→    // Get members to notify (excluding the author)\n   272→    const members = db\n   273→      .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id != ?\")\n   274→      .all(listingId, session.userId) as { user_id: number }[];\n   275→\n   276→    // Delete in a transaction\n   277→    const deleteTransaction = db.transaction(() => {\n   278→      // Notify members before deletion\n   279→      for (const member of members) {\n   280→        try {\n   281→          createNotification(\n   282→            member.user_id,\n   283→            listingId,\n   284→            \"listing_deleted\",\n   285→            `The reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" has been cancelled by the organizer.`\n   286→          );\n   287→        } catch {\n   288→          // Notification failure should not block deletion\n   289→        }\n   290→      }\n   291→\n   292→      // Delete related records first\n   293→      db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM listing_applications WHERE listing_id = ?\").run(listingId);\n   294→      db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\").run(listingId);\n   295→      db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM notifications WHERE listing_id = ?\").run(listingId);\n   296→      db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM ratings WHERE listing_id = ?\").run(listingId);\n   297→      // Delete the listing itself\n   298→      db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM listings WHERE id = ?\").run(listingId);\n   299→    });\n   300→\n   301→    deleteTransaction();\n   302→\n   303→    return NextResponse.json({ success: true });\n   304→  } catch (error) {\n   305→    console.error(\"Delete listing error:\", error);\n   306→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Internal server error\" }, { status: 500 });\n   307→  }\n   308→}\n   309→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_017Hd1VhCdyVw9bYdQVD7FVR","session_id":"6f88fa39-6af5-4e21-af20-53fcaa16208c","uuid":"ef8f366b-d1d6-444a-b7b8-97f7845f01fb","timestamp":"2026-03-23T13:02:24.189Z"}

useCallback } from \"react\";\n     4→import { useParams, useRouter } from \"next/navigation\";\n     5→import Image from \"next/image\";\n     6→\n     7→interface Member {\n     8→  id: number;\n display_name: string;\n    18→  bio: string;\n    19→  applied_at: string;\n    20→}\n    21→\n    22→interface Listing {\n    23→  id: number;\n    24→  author_id: number;\n    25→  book_title: string;\n    26→  book_author: string;\n    27→  book_cover_url: string;\n    28→  language: string;\n    29→  reading_pace: string;\n    30→  start_date: string;\n    31→  meeting_format: string;\n    32→  max_group_size: number;\n    33→  telegram_link: string;\n    34→  is_full: number;\n    35→  requires_approval: number;\n    36→  created_at: string;\n    37→  author_name: string;\n    38→  members: Member[];\n    39→  memberCount: number;\n    40→  isMember: boolean;\n    41→  isAuthor: boolean;\n    42→  hasApplied: boolean;\n    43→  applicationStatus: string;\n    44→  pendingApplicants: Applicant[];\n    45→  currentUserId: number | null;\n    46→  telegramBotConfigured: boolean;\n    47→  discordBotConfigured: boolean;\n    48→  platform_preference: string;\n    49→  discord_link: string;\n    50→}\n    51→\n    52→export default function ListingDetail() {\n    53→  const { id } = useParams();\n    54→  const router = useRouter();\n    55→  const [listing, setListing] = useState<Listing | null>(null);\n    56→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);\n    57→  const [joining, setJoining] = useState(false);\n    58→  const [applying, setApplying] = useState(false);\n    59→  const [decidingId, setDecidingId] = useState<number | null>(null);\n    60→  const [telegramLink, setTelegramLink] = useState(\"\");\n    61→  const [telegramSaving, setTelegramSaving] = useState(false);\n    62→  const [autoTelegramLoading, setAutoTelegramLoading] = useState(false);\n    63→  const [autoTelegramLink, setAutoTelegramLink] = useState(\"\");\n    64→  const [showManualFallback, setShowManualFallback] = useState(false);\n    65→  // Discord state\n    66→  const [discordLink, setDiscordLink] = useState(\"\");\n    67→  const [discordSaving, setGivenRatings] = useState<Record<number, { score: number; comment: string }>>({});\n    75→  const [ratingScores, setRatingScores] = useState<Record<number, number>>({});\n    76→  const [ratingComments, setRatingComments] = useState<Record<number, string>>({});\n    77→  const [ratingSaving, setRatingSaving] = useState<number | null>(null);\n    78→  const [ratingMessage, setRatingMessage] = useState(\"\");\n    79→\n    80→  const [notAvailable, setNotAvailable] = useState(false);\n    81→\n    82→  const [fetchError, setFetchError] = useState(false);\n    83→  const [showDeleteConfirm, setShowDeleteConfirm] = useState(false);\n    84→  const [deleting, setDeleting] = useState(false);\n    85→\n    86→  const fetchListing = async () => {\n    87→    try {\n    88→ setTelegramLink(data.listing.telegram_link || \"\");\n    93→        setDiscordLink(data.listing.discord_link || \"\");\n    94→      } else if (res.status === 404) {\n    95→
